  "decision": "fail",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Sling angle measured/estimated below 30 degrees from horizontal",
    "Resulting sling tension exceeds safe working load due to severe angle multiplier"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and clear area under suspended load",
    "Re-rig using longer slings or a spreader beam to achieve a minimum 30-degree angle, per OSHA 1926.251",
    "Re-inspect rigging components for overload damage before reuse"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926.251 and ASME B30 require sling angles not less than 30° from horizontal unless explicitly rated for lower angles. A sling angle under 30° imposes a tension multiplier >2.0, rapidly exceeding sling capacity and creating a high probability of catastrophic failure. Observed angle is visibly well below the minimum; therefore the rigging is non-compliant and unsafe at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)(4) - sling angle limitations",
    "ASME B30.9 - Slings safety standard"
  ]
}
